Controls.Tooltip: Your access is limited to the audit definitions for which you hold the position of team leader.

Controls.Caption: Planned Start Date
Controls.Tooltip: Specify the date when the audit activities will commence. This date marks the beginning of the audit execution phase.

Controls.Caption: Planned End Date
Controls.Tooltip: Specify the date when the audit activities will conclude. This date marks the end of the audit execution phase.

Controls.Caption: Actual Start Date
Controls.Tooltip: Specify the starting date for scheduling the audit. This date marks when the audit scheduling phase should begin to ensure a timely and organized audit execution.

Controls.Caption: Assign auditor(s) for each requirement.
Controls.Tooltip:
1. Click on Select All Requirements to assign the same auditor to all requirements
2. Click on Deselect All Requirements to uncheck all rows in the grid
3. To assign a different auditor per requirement, select in the grid only the requirements that will be assigned to the same auditor.

Controls.Caption: Selected Lead Collector will be auto-assigned to all requirements, but the user can still change the collector directly in the grid.
Assign the evidence collector(s) for each requirement
Controls.Tooltip:
1. Click on "Select All Evidence" to select all the Evidence. 
2. Click on Deselect All Evidence to uncheck all rows in the grid.
3. To assign a different collector per requirement, you have to select the collector directly from the grid and click on Save Changes.

Controls.Caption: Findings that are selected together in the corresponding dropdown below will create one quality event or CAPA instance.  To create separate instances, select the findings one by one and click on either the Create Quality Event or Create CAPA button after each selection. A finding can only be selected and triggered once.
QE / CAPA Reviewer
Controls.Tooltip: The selected user will receive the task to review either the quality event or CAPA created.
